testcase.name: test_lockout_after_5_failed
testcase.failure: backend/tests/test_p1.py:59: in test_lockout_after_5_failed assert r6.status_code == 429, f"expected 429 after lockout, got {r6.status_code}: {r6.text}" E AssertionError: expected 429 after lockout, got 401: {"detail":"Invalid email or password"} E assert 401 == 429 E + where 401 = <Response [401]>.status_code

testcase.name: test_successful_login_clears_attempts
testcase.failure: backend/tests/test_p1.py:80: in test_successful_login_clears_attempts assert r.status_code == 401 # still not locked because counter was reset ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ E assert 500 == 401 E + where 500 = <Response [500]>.status_code

testcase.name: test_status_enabled_after_setup

failure.message: AssertionError: assert 0 == 10 + where 0 = <built-in method get of dict object at 0xfc69138ae340>('backup_codes_remaining', 0) + where <built-in method get of dict object at 0xfc69138ae340> = {'backup_codes_remaining': 0, 'enabled': True}.get

testcase.failure: backend/tests/test_p1.py:123: in test_status_enabled_after_setup assert body.get("backup_codes_remaining", 0) == 10 E AssertionError: assert 0 == 10 E + where 0 = <built-in method get of dict object at 0xfc69138ae340>('backup_codes_remaining', 0) E + where <built-in method get of dict object at 0xfc69138ae340> = {'backup_codes_remaining': 0, 'enabled': True}.get
